relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Israel's tribes appeal to shared flesh and kinship as they approach David's throne.
Memory of leadership and Divine shepherd language becomes the basis of national coronation.
Elders and king form covenant, and David receives a broader anointing.
The narrator places David's age and reign within the architecture of royal memory.
Hebron and Jerusalem divide the forty years into regional and national rule.
The Jebusite city answers invasion with an ableist taunt, trusting its defenses against David.
David captures Zion, and conquered ground is renamed through the victor.
The response preserves hostility toward Jebusites and disabled bodies, language that sacred reading must refuse to reproduce as contempt.
The stronghold becomes a royal city through walls, earthworks, and occupation.
David's growing power is interpreted as accompaniment by YHWH of hosts.
Tyre's king supplies materials and skilled labor for a palace, binding diplomacy to royal construction.
David reads political consolidation as establishment for Israel's sake.
More women enter the royal household as wives and concubines while dynasty multiplies through their bodies.
The Jerusalem-born sons begin to be named.
The succession list continues, preserving sons while most mothers remain unnamed.
Three more sons complete the city's dynastic record.
Philistine forces respond to David's national anointing, and he withdraws to the stronghold.
An army occupies the Rephaim valley.
David seeks Divine authorization before entering battle and receives an assurance of victory.
The battlefield is remembered as a water-like breakthrough attributed to YHWH.
Philistine sacred images become abandoned property carried away by the victors.
The opposing force returns to the same valley.
This time David is told not to repeat the direct assault but to move around behind.
Sound in the treetops becomes a sign to act, joining landscape and military timing.
David follows the strategy and drives the Philistine force across a wide corridor.

contemplative_note: A city can become sacred in later memory while still having been someone else's inhabited home. The chapter asks readers to separate Divine presence from ableist contempt, conquest, royal accumulation, and the silencing of women whose bodies sustain a dynasty.
